title: New EU rules on pay transparency explained
content: New EU pay transparency rules are now taking effect across the EU. They will increase pay transparency, strengthen enforcement of the principle of equal pay between women and men, and improve access to justice for victims of pay discrimination.

title: EDF’s first-ever FSTP sub-call attracts strong interest from newcomers to EU defence funding
content: The submission phase of the first-ever Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP) sub-call under the European Defence Fund (EDF)-funded MAJoR project has successfully concluded, attracting strong interest from innovative small and medium-sized companies across Europe.

title: EBRD and EU step up support for digital and green investments
content: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) and the European Union (EU) are stepping up their support for the green transition across the Western Balkans, and digital development across the regions outside the EU where the EBRD operates.

title: EU fisheries: fishing sustainability improves, but challenges remain
content: Today, the Commission published its annual communication on the state of EU fisheries, taking stock of progress towards sustainable fishing and setting the course for the upcoming cycle of fishing opportunities for 2027.

title: Commission endorses Lithuania’s €884 million Social Climate Plan to support vulnerable households and micro-enterprises in the clean transition
content: The European Commission has adopted Lithuania’s Social Climate Plan, the second one under the new Social Climate Fund.
snippet: The European Commission has adopted Lithuania’s Social Climate Plan, the second one under the new Social Climate Fund.

title: Commission approves €100 million Austrian State aid scheme to support cleantech manufacturing capacity
content: The European Commission has approved a €100 million Austrian scheme to support clean technology ('cleantech') manufacturing capacity, in line with the objectives of the Clean Industrial Deal. This measure will contribute to the transition towards a net-zero economy.

title: Commission adopts temporary adjustments to Basel III Market Risk Rules to safeguard EU banks’ competitiveness
content: To ensure that the EU banking sector benefits from a level playing field with international competitors, the European Commission has today introduced targeted, time-limited amendments to the EU’s implementation of the Fundamental Review of the Trading Book (FRTB).

title: Read-out by President von der Leyen following her phone call with Prime Minister of Armenia Nikol Pashinyan
content: Today I spoke with Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan about Russia's recent restrictions targeting Armenia.
snippet: Today I spoke with Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan about Russia's recent restrictions targeting Armenia.

title: New EMN inform examines alternatives to providing housing in-kind for applicants for international protection
content: From financial allowances to service provision and monitoring, find out how EMN Member and Observer countries, as well as Norway and Serbia are using alternatives to in‑kind housing to support applicants for international protection.

title: EU-Moldova Investment Conference announces up to €641 million in strategic investments
content: At the EU-Moldova Investment Conference, EU Commissioner for Enlargement, Marta Kos announced investment plans and project initiatives worth up to €641 million in coordination with international financial institutions, private sector partners and public stakeholders.

title: Commission approves €25 million Spanish State aid for fishing companies facing increased fuel prices
content: The European Commission has approved a €25 million Spanish State aid scheme to support fishing companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.
snippet: The European Commission has approved a €25 million Spanish State aid scheme to support fishing companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.

title: June infringements package: key decisions on energy
content: In its regular package of infringement decisions, the European Commission pursues legal action against Member States for failing to comply with their obligations under EU law.
snippet: In its regular package of infringement decisions, the European Commission pursues legal action against Member States for failing to comply with their obligations under EU law.

title: President von der Leyen at the EU-Western Balkans Summit in Montenegro to reaffirm EU's commitment to the region*
content: Today and tomorrow, President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Council António Costa and President of the European Parliament Roberta Metsola will be in Tivat, Montenegro, to participate in the EU-Western Balkans Summit.

title: European Energy Exchange appointed as EU ETS common auction platform from 2027
content: On 2 June, the European Commission signed a five-year contract with European Energy Exchange AG (EEX) and its clearing system European Commodity Clearing AG (ECC) for the services of the fourth common auction platform under the EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS).

title: Commissioner Roswall meets young Europeans to discuss a resilient nature-based future
content: The Youth Policy Dialogue in Brussels brought together 13 young European leaders to discuss how investments in nature can drive both environmental protection and economic resilience.
snippet: The Youth Policy Dialogue in Brussels brought together 13 young European leaders to discuss how investments in nature can drive both environmental protection and economic resilience.

title: Flagship projects on AI for grids and data centre sustainability

title: Discover the EU projects boosting resilience in your region

title: Amazonian Indigenous women win 2026 #ForOurPlanet prize
content: The award highlights the initiative's commitment to strengthening the leadership of Indigenous women in protecting the Amazon rainforest.
snippet: The award highlights the initiative's commitment to strengthening the leadership of Indigenous women in protecting the Amazon rainforest.

title: EU Steel Regulation: Commission launches consultation on the type of evidence to be provided on country of ‘melt and pour’
content: Today, the European Commission launched a targeted consultation seeking stakeholders’ feedback on the appropriate documentation for implementing the country ‘melt and pour’ traceability requirements under the EU Steel Regulation.

title: 2026 Spring Semester Package sets out guidance on fairer labour markets and improved social protection
content: The 2026 European Semester Spring Package presents new guidelines regarding employment and social policies, with recommendations on human capital (investing in people and their skills), improving job quality and reducing poverty.

title: Eurobarometer shows continued public support for biodiversity protection across Europe

title: Experts call for placing ecological sustainability at the core of EU policies
content: The Ethics Advice Mechanism published an Opinion recognising ecological sustainability as key to Europe’s resilience, prosperity, security and democracy.

title: Executive Vice-President Ribera and Commissioner Jørgensen in Finland to discuss clean energy transition and housing affordability

title: Commission proposes tech sovereignty package to strengthen Europe's digital autonomy and resilience
content: The European Commission today presented the European Technological Sovereignty Package, a set of measures to strengthen Europe's capacity in semiconductors, artificial intelligence (AI), cloud and open source.

title: Spring semester package: steering EU economies to increased competitiveness
content: The European Commission has set out policy guidance for EU countries to help them revitalise the EU's competitiveness. The guidance also steers EU countries along a path to greater strategic autonomy, economic and social resilience and cohesion, while maintaining fiscal sustainability.
